## [15:14 - 18:16]

it's also great if you have a current business, but you have a new offer you want to come out with. You want sort of help creating that product. You want to test out an offer. And I still use seed launches to this day. I did one just a few months ago. It was so much fun. I loved doing it. So, anyways, you do, you put out a couple small pieces of pre-launch content and then the way you make your offer is that you're actually going to deliver it via a series of calls. So, let's say you were going to teach guitar and you know the message in here in the pre-launch could be people have been asking me, you know, I've been, I've been publishing on my blog. I've been publishing on Facebook about, you know, tips about how to learn guitar. People keep on asking me if they would just go ahead and teach it. So, what I've decided to do is I put together a series of tele-seminars or webinars you can pick, whichever one you want. You put together a series of tele-seminars and then you deliver your content over three tele-seminars. You could do five if you want, if you got a more in-depth topic and you need five calls to cover it, but you could just do three calls and you just, what you do is you make this offer. Generally, what you're looking for is, you know, it'd be ideal if you get 20 or 30 people in your program and I'll explain why, because what you do is after they buy from you, they go through your little mini sideways sales letter and then they buy from you, then you, the first thing you do is you send them to a survey and the survey you're asking them what they need to know about the topic and you know, it could be just like, hey, I'm really excited about this program, I'm getting ready to start teaching it, I just need to make sure that I cover, you know, every time, they cover this topic completely, could you do me a favor and just tell me, if you're going to sit down with me for lunch or for, or for coffee, what would be the top two questions you would ask me? So, you send your people through that survey, those questions tell you what you need to cover in your product. So, after that first survey, you could just take a look at it, take all the questions that everyone sent in and group them into three main topics, those three topics are your calls. So, then you do a, then you do your first call and you're basically getting on there and just answering the questions people have asked you. Then after that call, you run them through another survey that's basically says, hey, that was a great call, love, love teaching that, love being on the call with you, great interaction. I just have a couple questions for you. Is there anything that isn't 100% clear about that, you know, what I covered on that last call? So, that's the first question. So, the second question is, our next call is going to be about X, whatever it's going to be about. What are your top two questions about that topic? And then that tells you what you need to cover in that next call. And then you just keep on doing that, survey call, survey call for as many calls as you want to do. Now, one of the things that will make all the difference for your business is that you always want to over deliver. This is something I absolutely obsess about. So, like, it's really easy in the seed launch to over deliver by just doing a bonus call that you don't announce up front.

## [18:16 - 21:19]

You don't announce it until you're somewhere in between the first and the second call. You just say, hey, by the way, I told you you're going to get three calls, but we're actually going to have a bonus call. The bonus call is going to be the best call of them all. You're going to love it. And then after this, after your final regular call, the call you promised, then you send them to another survey. So, you know, I just want to know if there's any questions, whatsoever, we're going to wide open Q&A in this next call. And then you send them as the bonus call. That, the whole thing, this whole entire process, because there's so much interaction, because there's so many surveys. And in most of these calls, you can actually take live questions. I love taking live questions on my call. Because you're doing that all the way through, you are going to create a great product. A lot of people worry about creating products, and they worry that the product's not going to be good enough, or that it's not going to be perfect. You end up with very, very happy clients. You generally end up with great testimonials. You can usually end up with great case studies. And then because you're recording these calls all the way through, you end up with the recordings.
